title: Treasury Launches the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Innovation Series
summary: The Office of the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) and Treasury's AI Transformation Office (AITO) launched the AI Innovation Series on March 23, 2026—a public-private initiative consisting of four roundtables to explore AI use cases in financial services. The initiative focuses on balancing AI adoption with safety and soundness, with Treasury Secretary Bessent signaling a shift from 'constraint' to enabling 'productivity-enhancing technology' adoption.
key_points: FSOC and Treasury's AITO launched the AI Innovation Series on March 23, 2026, consisting of four roundtables convening financial institutions, tech firms, and regulators

Treasury Secretary Bessent stated the approach is 'optimizing regulation to support growth' and moving from 'constraint' toward recognizing 'failure to adopt productivity-enhancing technology as its own risk'
Deputy Assistant Secretary Skinner stated that when institutions cannot deploy AI tools for fraud detection and credit allocation, 'the system becomes less efficient and less secure'
Chief AI Officer Paras Malik stated the priority is 'operationalization, embedding AI into core workflows' and ensuring 'governance frameworks evolve alongside deployment'
The initiative addresses AI embedded in fraud detection, cybersecurity, credit underwriting, and operational risk management
Treasury stated it will continue evaluating 'regulatory frameworks and enforcement policies' for AI adoption while preserving national security
why_it_matters: Consider softening 'signals a regulatory posture shift' to 'announces a potential regulatory posture shift' since no actual policy changes have occurred yet—the initiative is exploratory with no confirmed outcomes.
